issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The html element has scroll-behavior: smooth, but the :focus-visible rule on line 345 sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement like box-shadow or a border. This prevents keyboard users from seeing their focus position when the page or a fragment identifier is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link explicitly removes the focus outline on :focus-visible but does not provide a box-shadow or border alternative. Keyboard users will have no visual indication when this link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline on :focus-visible without a visible alternative. This blocks keyboard-only users from identifying the interactive state of the menu trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 548: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links remove the focus outline via the global :focus-visible reset on line 345, and no specific replacement is defined for these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links set outline: none on focus-visible. While they have an outline-offset, the zero-width outline remains invisible, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 638: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline on :focus. While it adds a box-shadow, the box-shadow uses a low-opacity blue (rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2)) which may not meet the visibility requirements for a focus indicator replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 668: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing any replacement style in this specific rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 675: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class removes the focus outline on :focus-visible without providing a box-shadow or border replacement in the same rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 455: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline on :focus-visible without a visible replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which card is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 845: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus outline on :focus-visible without a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline on :focus-visible without a visible alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table region is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes the focus outline on :focus-visible without a replacement, failing to signal focus to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the default outline on :focus. The provided box-shadow (rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2)) is too faint to serve as a valid focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1375: .form-group input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs explicitly remove the focus outline on :focus-visible without providing a replacement style.

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px and the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 495: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons have a min-width and min-height of 20px, which is too small for reliable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a height of 28px. While it meets the 24px minimum, it fails the 44px recommendation for touch targets without sufficient surrounding space.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: #search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is significantly below the 44px recommended target size for mobile users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, making them difficult to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 945: .filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: .pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product CTA buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: .product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; margin-top: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class on line 405 does not hide content from AT, but the .sr-only implementation itself is incorrect for accessible hiding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 375: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for its reveal animation, but there is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); position: absolute; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transform transition for the :active state (translateY), but this motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex perspective transform and transition on hover, which can cause vestibular distress. The reduced-motion block on line 805 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 805: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform: translateY transition on hover, but the reduced-motion override on line 855 is incomplete as it only targets the card and not the link arrow animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 855: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card, .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which crowds text and makes it significantly harder for users with dyslexia or low vision to distinguish individual words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces the legibility of body text by decreasing the space between characters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 216: body { letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use negative letter-spacing (via var(--letter-spacing-tight)), which can impair readability for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 225: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero H1 uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly degrades character recognition at large font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745: .hero-content h1 { letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration, and many links (e.g., in .hero-note, .product-meta, .footer-brand) do not provide a non-colour differentiator like an underline or bold weight, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: a { text-decoration: underline; } .prose a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: font-size below 16px on body selectors
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. Since body text and many components use rem units based on this root size, the resulting text is significantly smaller than the 16px (1rem) readability standard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 185: html { font-size: 16px; }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1445: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
Line 1458: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the layout mode between grid and list but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. Users of assistive technology will not know which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the `applyFilters` function updates the grid but does not announce the result of the filtering (e.g., "Showing 12 products") to a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module applies an error class and shows an error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3415: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as successful, the `_renderResult` function removes error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3409: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for `mobileNav` does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger trigger. Screen readers may not announce the button as a toggle until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module hides the header on scroll-down by adding a CSS class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. This leaves the hidden header content reachable by some assistive technologies while visually obscured.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger is not programmatically linked to the drawer element it controls via the `aria-controls` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1516: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || 'nav-drawer');`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 1559: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTabClick` function for product filter tabs updates the active class but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. This makes it impossible for non-sighted users to determine which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive)); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); });`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible count of products is updated in the `_updateCountBadge` function, but no live region announcement is triggered. Users of assistive technology will not know how many results remain after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `_countBadge.textContent = String(count); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not signal that the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function clears error messages when a field becomes valid but does not reset or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. The field will continue to be announced as "invalid" even after the user corrects the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2710: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the navigation drawer but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. The initial state of the toggle is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update` function for the scroll-to-top button toggles the `hidden` attribute but does not manage `aria-hidden`. While `hidden` works in many browsers, `aria-hidden="true"` is required for robust removal from the accessibility tree during the fade transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open(), mobileNav.close(), and mobileNav.toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2085: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 2095: modify toggle() to update aria-expanded based on new state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and faqAccordion.close() functions expand/collapse FAQ answers without updating the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users lose track of which questions are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3065: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle does not announce change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function switches between monthly/annual billing but does not announce the new selection to screen readers. Users who rely on auditory feedback may not know the pricing display has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Monthly billing selected');` (or equivalent for annual).

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the filter result to screen readers. Users cannot perceive how many items match the active filter without manually exploring the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) || `${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() marks fields invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: in the else block (when result.valid is false), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the valid block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded initial state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.init() does not set aria-expanded="false" on the nav toggle button when the drawer is initially closed. Screen‑reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040: after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded initial state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu.register() does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when the panels are initially hidden. Assistive technologies cannot convey the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120: after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria-expanded initial state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.init() does not set aria-expanded on FAQ trigger buttons based on the initial open/closed state. Screen‑reader users are not informed which answers are initially expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020: in the loop that applies initial ARIA states, ensure `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, item.open);` is called for each item.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button visibility changes not reflected in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: scrollToTop._update() hides/shows the button using the hidden attribute and CSS classes but does not update aria-hidden. When hidden, the button remains in the accessibility tree, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: when setting `_btn.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, also set `_btn.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`. Line 1965: when removing hidden, also set `_btn.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or remove the aria-hidden attribute.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria-expanded initial state on page load

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close/toggle functions change the visual state of the drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3005: In open() add: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 3020: In close() add: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initial state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" on page load when the drawer is closed. Screen reader users are not informed of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2985: In init() after _trigger is assigned, add: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded initial state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads and panels are hidden. Screen reader users are not informed of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3160: In register() after setting aria-haspopup, add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: View mode toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle button (grid/list) changes visual state but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2810: In _onViewToggle() add: btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', mode === 'list' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count changes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users with visual impairments cannot perceive the dynamic content change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2730: In applyFilters() after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(`Now showing ${totalMatches} products.`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (month/year), no announcement is made to screen readers. Users with visual impairments cannot perceive the dynamic price updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3420: In setPeriod() after _syncToggleState(period), add: liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${period} billing.`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the invalid input fields are not marked with aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not informed that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2510: In _renderResult() else block (invalid case), add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); Line 2515: In valid case, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown/hidden but aria-hidden is not updated. Screen readers may still read the drawer content when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3005: In open() add: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); Line 3020: In close() add: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels do not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown/hidden but aria-hidden is not updated. Screen readers may still read the panel content when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3205: In _openInstance() add: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); Line 3220: In _closeInstance() add: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid input becomes valid, aria-invalid is not cleared or set to "false". Screen reader users may continue to hear error states for corrected fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: In _renderResult() valid case, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` changes the drawer's visibility but does not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Screen-reader users won't know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2187: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open);` after `_open = true;` and Line 2190: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open);` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` changes the billing period but does not update `aria-pressed` on the toggle switch. Screen-reader users won't know which option is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2807: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year');` after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in `pricing` updates price displays but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen-reader users won't know prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2844: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` after `_pricingState.period = period;` (and similar for monthly)

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` shows error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. Screen readers won't announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3476: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);` and Line 3478: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the nav toggle button when the page loads. Screen readers may report an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2182: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-controls` only if missing, but doesn't ensure the panel has an ID. Without `aria-controls`, screen readers can't associate trigger and panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2306: Add `if (!panel.id) panel.id = srUtils.generateId('dropdown-panel');` before setting `aria-controls`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `_lockScroll` and `_unlockScroll` functions in `mobileNav` change body styles but don't manage `aria-hidden` on the drawer when hidden. This can leave hidden content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2197: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` in `_lockScroll` and Line 2198: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` in `_unlockScroll`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector removes the default focus outline without providing a visible alternative (like a box-shadow or border). outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector removes the focus outline for non-keyboard users, but if the browser's default behavior is suppressed here, it may interfere with accessibility expectations for certain input methods.
issuesFound.fix: Line 324: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; } (Note: This is generally acceptable if :focus-visible is robust, but the global removal of outlines is risky).

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none;` and only offsets the outline, providing no visible focus indicator for any keyboard‑focused element. This blocks access for keyboard‑only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the mobile navigation drawer but never set or update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')`; in `close()` set `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden drawer not reflected in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code only removes CSS classes and hides the overlay but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element (`_drawer`). Hidden elements should be removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')`; in `open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick` and `_onTriggerKeydown` the dropdown is opened/closed by adding/removing CSS classes, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening a dropdown call `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')`; after closing call `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded on keyboard interaction
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard handler for the dropdown (`_onTriggerKeydown`) toggles the panel but does not modify `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, causing a mismatch between visual state and ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix: Mirror the fix from Issue 3 for the keyboard path.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so screen readers do not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult` when `result.valid` is false, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error classes but does not remove `aria-invalid`. The attribute may remain, incorrectly indicating an error.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult` when `result.valid` is true, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge (`_countBadge`) is updated, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new count to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and update its text with the new count each time `_updateCountBadge` runs.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header does not set aria‑expanded/aria-hidden on toggle elements
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header adds/removes CSS classes on scroll but does not update any ARIA attributes on elements that become hidden (e.g., the header when it hides). Hidden elements should have `aria-hidden="true"` to be removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: When the header is hidden (`_hidden = true`), call `_header.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')`; when shown, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list renderer does not set aria‑live on updates
issuesFound.explanation: The autocomplete suggestion list (`suggestionListRenderer.render`) updates the list content but does not announce changes via an ARIA live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware that new suggestions have appeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and update its text (e.g., “5 suggestions available”) each time `render` is called.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not have aria‑hidden when closed (duplicate of Issue 2 but for initial state)
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the drawer is hidden via CSS but lacks `aria-hidden="true"`, so it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In the module initialization, set `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` before any interaction.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle element missing aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button (`_toggleEl`) controls the pricing cards but does not have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the controlled region’s ID, reducing discoverability for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Assign an ID to the pricing container (e.g., `id="pricing-cards"`), then set `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-controls','pricing-cards')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the drawer but never resets aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the code only removes CSS classes; it never adds aria‑hidden="true" to the drawer element, so the hidden navigation remains focusable for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() opens a dropdown but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the state invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() closes a dropdown but never resets aria‑expanded to false on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed the panel is only hidden with CSS; it never receives aria‑hidden="true", so assistive technologies may still expose its contents.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an item but never sets aria‑expanded on the question button, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which item is open.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
function open(item, announceToSR) {
  …
  setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); // ← add this line
  …
}
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() collapses an item but never resets aria‑expanded to false.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
function close(item, announceToSR) {
  …
  setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add this line
  …
}
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed the panel is hidden with hidden attribute only; it never receives aria‑hidden="true", leaving its content potentially reachable by assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period‑selection buttons in pricing.setPeriod() are styled as active/inactive but never reflect their state with aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle groups.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing toggle does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “monthly/annual” switch element _toggleEl toggles a CSS class but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving its state ambiguous to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
Product filter does not announce result count
Pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation:
pricing.setPeriod() updates price labels but does not announce the new billing period, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the change.
  Fix (add at end of setPeriod()):
```js
liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation:
mobileNav.init() does not set aria‑expanded="false" on the hamburger button, so the attribute is missing on page load.
  Fix (inside init() after locating _trigger):
```js
if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add this line
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation:
dropdownMenu.register() creates triggers but never sets an initial aria‑expanded="false".
  Fix (inside register() after creating inst):
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add this line
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation:
faqRegistry.parse() creates FAQ triggers but does not set aria‑expanded="false" when the item is collapsed.
  Fix (inside parse() after creating trigger):
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(isInitiallyOpen));
```
*(If isInitiallyOpen is false the attribute will be "false".)*

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden elements not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation:
The hide/show functions only toggle CSS classes; they never set aria‑hidden on the element being hidden.
  Fix (example for mobileNav.close):
```js
function close(announceToSR) {
  …
  _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); // ← add this line
  …
}
function open(announceToSR) {
  …
  _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');      // ← add this line
  …
}
```
(Apply analogous changes to _dropdownMenu._closeInstance_ and _faqAccordion.close_ to keep the UI and accessibility tree in sync.)

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for all keyboard users across the entire site, making navigation impossible for those who rely on keyboard focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: .btn focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none but does not define a box-shadow or border color change. Since the base .btn class has a transparent border, keyboard users will see no visual indication when a button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating the header will not see which link is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule removes the outline without adding a box-shadow or border. This hides focus state from keyboard users trying to open the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with no visible alternative. Users navigating dropdown menus via keyboard will lose track of their position.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule removes the outline without a box-shadow or border replacement. The search button will appear unfocused even when active via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focused links in the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without replacement. Since the link covers the card, the lack of focus ring makes the entire card's interactivity unclear to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a box-shadow or border. Users cannot see which filter is currently focused when navigating via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without replacement. This wrapper is focusable (tabindex="0" implied by context) to allow scrolling, but keyboard users won't see when it is active.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule removes the outline without a visible alternative. Users cannot identify which question is focused before expanding it.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a box-shadow or border change. Keyboard users cannot see when a checkbox is focused.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without replacement. Links in the footer brand section will not show focus state to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard navigation in the footer is inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Social link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without replacement. Social media links will not indicate focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24px (SC 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons need larger targets to be accessible for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. This makes it difficult for users with limited dexterity to toggle the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text, exposing it to sighted users and cluttering the layout.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 743 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D rotation effect.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition to slide into view. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This can significantly degrade readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. Many links (e.g., in footer, account nav) rely solely on color changes on hover, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 213: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) controls the visibility of the nav drawer, but the `open()` and `close()` functions only toggle CSS classes and emit events. They do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware of the menu's state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not activate focus trap on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function reveals the navigation drawer but fails to activate the focus trap (`_trap.activate()`). This allows keyboard focus to escape the modal drawer to the underlying page content, violating focus management requirements for modal dialogs.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not update aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller toggles the `hidden` attribute on the panel but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed when the dropdown opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the code sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. The initial state of the toggle is not exposed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function sets `item.open = true` and removes `hidden` from the panel, but it does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function does update it, creating an inconsistent state where opening the accordion is not announced.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion list. The `_open()` and `_close()` functions update the internal state `_searchState.open` but do not update `aria-expanded` on `_inputEl`, preventing screen readers from knowing if the suggestion list is visible.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the search input is wired with `aria-haspopup="listbox"` but `aria-expanded` is never set. The initial closed state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tabs update their visual active state via CSS classes but do not update `aria-pressed`. This violates the requirement for toggle buttons to expose their state via ARIA.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility adds an `aria-describedby` reference to the error message but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Similarly, `markValid` removes the description but does not clear `aria-invalid`. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visibility changes not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The header visibility is toggled via CSS classes (`headerHidden`) and potentially `display` styles, but the code does not update `aria-hidden` on the header element. When hidden, the header remains in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is shown/hidden by toggling the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes, but `aria-hidden` is not synchronized. When visually hidden, it may still be focusable or announced by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated dynamically when filters are applied, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users will not know how many results are currently shown without manual exploration.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The savings message (e.g., "Save 20% with annual billing") is updated via `textContent` when the billing period changes, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the new discount information.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.

Line 1406: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1433: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size to 12px, making the root em unit 12px. This causes all rem-based text sizing to be smaller than user defaults, resulting in body text that is approximately 12px (below the 16px minimum recommended for readability). Users with low vision or presbyopia will struggle to read content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: font-size: 100%; /* 16px default */

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height too tight for readability
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements set line-height to 1, which is well below the WCAG 1.4.8 recommended minimum of 1.5. This dense spacing makes text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and increases eye strain.
issuesFound.fix: Line 337: line-height: var(--line-height-normal); /* 1.5 */

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em, compressing words together. Negative word-spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia or those using screen magnification, as words may visually merge.
issuesFound.fix: Line 324: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removal without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The anchor element selector sets text-decoration to none globally, removing underlines from all links. Without an alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or background highlight), users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 331: /* Remove this rule or add */ text-decoration: underline; /* Alternatively, add border-bottom on hover/focus states */

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible. This breaks the utility's purpose of providing content only to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383-393: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transition property including transform with a 500ms duration, and the :hover state applies a perspective transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial animation, which may cause vestibular disorders in sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 799-807: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card has a transition including transform with a 200ms duration, and the :hover state applies translateY(-3px). There is no reduced-motion override to disable this lift animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1044-1054: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on FAQ items
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item has transitions for border-color and box-shadow that create visual motion when expanding/collapsing. While subtle, these should be disabled for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1339-1341: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button has transitions for background-color and color. While not spatial motion, the color transitions should respect reduced motion preferences for consistency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1344-1346: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses background-color var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), producing a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1. This fails WCAG AA for normal text (requires 4.5:1) and is only acceptable for large text (18px+) or bold text (14px+ bold). Since the font-size is var(--font-size-base) (12px), this fails accessibility requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); /* #004aaa provides 7:1 contrast */ or increase font-weight to 700 and font-size to var(--font-size-lg);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary uses background-color #5588bb with white text (#ffffff), yielding approximately 3.5:1 contrast. With a font-size of 12px (1rem based on html font-size: 12px), this fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); /* #004aaa */ or add font-weight: 700; font-size: var(--font-size-lg); /* 18px */

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stats section values
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-value uses white text (#ffffff) on a gradient background ranging from #5588bb to #bb88ee. Against #5588bb, the contrast is approximately 3.0:1, failing WCAG AA for large text (requires 3:1) and normal text (requires 4.5:1). Against the lighter #bb88ee, contrast is even worse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: color: var(--colour-neutral-900); /* Dark text on light gradient */ or add text-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); and ensure background is darkened.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stats section labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255,255,255,0.3) (30% opacity white) on the gradient background, resulting in extremely low contrast (below 2:1), making the text nearly invisible for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1152: color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9); /* 90% opacity for ~7:1 contrast */

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter section text
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter paragraph uses rgba(255,255,255,0.8) on a gradient background from #5588bb to #bb88ee. Against the lighter purple sections, this produces insufficient contrast below the 4.5:1 required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1384: color: var(--colour-neutral-0); /* Solid white for maximum contrast */

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint in the newsletter section uses rgba(255,255,255,0.65), which is approximately 4.2:1 contrast against the darker blue (#5588bb) but fails against the lighter purple sections of the gradient, dropping below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1423: color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9); /* Ensure 7:1 contrast across entire gradient */

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 416: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and uses transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion, which can cause issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 608: (add after line 647) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform transitions and 3D transforms. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1157 is empty and does not disable these animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1157: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Feature card transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card class includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover. The media query at line 1288 only disables the hover transform but does not remove the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1288: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after selector includes transform transitions. The media query at line 1348 only disables the hover transform but does not remove the transition property itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1348: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } .feature-card a:hover::after { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Stat item transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item class includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover. The media query at line 1288 (actually 1782) only disables the hover transform but does not disable the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1782: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card class includes transform in its transition and uses transform on hover. The media query at line 1949 only disables the hover transform but does not remove the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1949: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and violates best practices for text spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 185: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) against a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, well below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1801: `.stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger selector uses background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) with color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 710: `.btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none from the global a rule and only changes color on hover without adding an underline or other non-colour indicator. Links must be distinguishable by more than just colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 973: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); text-decoration: none; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }` and Line 981: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector inherits text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover without adding an underline or border-bottom. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2548: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }` and Line 2558: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector inherits text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover. Social links must have a non-colour visual indicator such as underline or border to distinguish them from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2591: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }` and Line 2601: `.social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for body text. This requires users to zoom more to read content comfortably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 163: `font-size: 16px;`

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 minimum. This tight spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 203: `line-height: var(--line-height-normal);`
