issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls and initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without aria-controls linking it to the drawer element, and without aria-expanded="false" set initially. This prevents screen readers from understanding the relationship between the button and the drawer, and the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1433: After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add:
  `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer');`
  `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`
  `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manipulate the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This leaves screen reader users unaware of the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1484: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the end of _openInstance (after inst.open = true).
  Line 1496: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of _closeInstance (after the guard clause).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger, but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" for the collapsed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1590: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add:
  `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function adds the open class and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but unlike the close function, it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This creates an asymmetry where the state is only communicated when closing, not when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2447: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded on input element
issuesFound.explanation: The site search suggestion list is shown and hidden via _open and _close functions, but the combobox input element (_inputEl) does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated to reflect whether the suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2182: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the _open function.
  Line 2196: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the _close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the page loads and the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2280: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add:
  `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle the active category but only update CSS classes. They do not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen reader users, which is required for toggle buttons that remain pressed when activated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2734: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with:
  `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`
  `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) update their active CSS class but do not set aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2994: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with:
  `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`
  `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1129: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1147: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function (after the if-else block).

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates in the DOM, but this change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed that the filter action was successful or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2660: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
  `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing \${totalMatches} product\${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly vs annual), the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3048: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add:
  `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button trigger controls the visibility of the navigation drawer, but the code toggles CSS classes without synchronising the aria-expanded state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2338 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`
  Line 2352 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded and aria-controls initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false" on page load, and the aria-controls attribute is never set to reference the drawer element. Assistive technologies cannot infer the relationship or initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2398 (after `_drawer = qs(...)`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`
  Line 2399: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: A focus trap variable is declared but never instantiated or activated when the menu opens, allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer. Additionally, focus is not returned to the trigger on close.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2342 (in open function): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`
  Line 2365 (in close function): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code removes CSS classes but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. Although CSS may hide it, explicit aria-hidden ensures it is excluded from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2360 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line 2344 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons open and close panels via these functions, but aria-expanded is never toggled to reflect the current state. Screen readers cannot convey whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2458 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2470 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2554 (after setting aria-controls): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded programmatically or via user interaction, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". The close function sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4124 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion listbox, but the aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the listbox visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3394 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3406 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls, but aria-expanded="false" is not set initially when the suggestion panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520 (after mounting listbox): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function associates an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1923 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared or marked valid, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving stale invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1938 (in markValid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count and results change, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the grid has updated or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3078 (after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen readers do not inform users of the price change or savings message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3695 (after _updateSavingsMessage): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged ? STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged(period) : \`Prices switched to \${period} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens or closes, no status message is announced. Users navigating via screen reader do not receive confirmation that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2346 (in open): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 2368 (in close): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus updates the DOM but does not announce the state change via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2462 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 2476 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

fpTitles: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria-expanded when panels are opened or closed. The state change is only conveyed visually via CSS classes, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a FAQ item, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (though it is correctly set to "false" when closing). This creates an inconsistent state where screen readers report the item as collapsed even when content is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` at the beginning of the function before calling addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors from a field, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (set it to "false" or remove it). The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count updates visually and emits a custom event, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match their selection or if the filter returned no results.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Add `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products found' : STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(totalMatches) : 'Showing ${totalMatches} products');` inside the filterAnimation.run callback after _updateCountBadge.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods (Monthly/Annual), prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing' : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing');` before the `emit` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcements for menu state
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions accept an announceToSR parameter that is never used. Menu open/close state changes are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether the navigation is accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` before emitting the event. Line mobileNav.close: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before emitting the event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen readers may assume the menu is expanded by default since the attribute is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After `_initialised = true;` and the existence check for _trigger, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Newly registered dropdown triggers are not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Since the panel is hidden by default, the trigger should reflect this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Screen readers may not correctly identify the collapsed state of the suggestions list on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: After `_initialised = true;` and the check for _inputEl, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, the drawer is hidden via CSS class removal only. It remains in the accessibility tree (not aria-hidden or display:none), allowing screen reader users to navigate to invisible content.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing. Correspondingly, in mobileNav.open, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav background not hidden when menu open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, the background content (main, footer) remains accessible to screen readers. It should be aria-hidden to prevent navigation behind the modal. The close function should remove this.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: Add `setAttr(qs('main'), 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and `setAttr(qs('footer'), 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (or use a wrapper). Line mobileNav.close: Add code to remove these aria-hidden attributes.

issuesFound.title: contactForm inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for first name, last name, and email do not have HTML autocomplete attributes set. This prevents browsers from autofilling user data and hinders accessibility for users with cognitive disabilities who rely on autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line contactForm.init: Inside the CONTACT_FIELDS.forEach loop, after binding validation, add autocomplete attributes: for `[name="full_name"]` use `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');`, for `[name="email"]` use `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');` (split full_name into first/last if possible, or use 'name').

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from suggesting the user's email address.
issuesFound.fix: Line newsletterForm.init: After `if (_emailEl) { inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']); }`, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

mobileNav background not hidden when menu open
contactForm inputs missing autocomplete attributes
newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between the label and its corresponding input field, preventing screen readers from announcing the label when the input is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1222: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid prop
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style error states but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of the field to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose it to assistive technologies via aria-invalid. Users with screen readers will not be notified when the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1256: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component receives an invalid prop for visual error indication but fails to set aria-invalid on the select element. This prevents screen readers from announcing the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1281: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
